# Dr Tutor

> Dr Tutor is a UK-based online science tutoring service for students aged 7–18 (KS2 through A-Levels). All tutors are PGCE-qualified science specialists. The service offers personalised one-to-one online lessons, real-time progress tracking, comprehensive free study resources, and transparent monthly pricing with no hidden fees. Parents can book a free assessment to get started.

## Key Facts

- Website: https://www.drtutor.uk
- Location: United Kingdom (online, nationwide)
- Subjects: Science (Biology, Chemistry, Physics), with additional support in English and Maths
- Age Range: KS2 (ages 7–11), KS3 (ages 11–14), GCSE (ages 14–16), A-Level (ages 16–18), 11+ Preparation
- Tutors: All PGCE-qualified science specialists
- Delivery: Online one-to-one lessons via Zoom / Google Meet
- Teaching Framework: Doug Lemov's "Teach Like a Champion", delivered through a structured I Do → We Do → You Do cycle
- Free Assessment: https://platform.drtutor.uk/register
- Contact: https://www.drtutor.uk/contact

## Pages

- [Home](https://www.drtutor.uk/): Landing page with overview of services, teaching methodology, tutor profiles, testimonials, and FAQs
- [Pricing](https://www.drtutor.uk/pricing): Transparent monthly packages for all key stages, add-on sessions, and trust guarantees
- [Resources](https://www.drtutor.uk/resources): Free downloadable science revision guides, topic summaries, and study materials for KS2 to A-Level
- [Contact](https://www.drtutor.uk/contact): Get in touch via form, email, or WhatsApp; book a free assessment
- [Terms & Conditions](https://www.drtutor.uk/terms): Service agreement, privacy policy, and cancellation terms
- [Book Free Assessment](https://platform.drtutor.uk/register): Registration page to book a free initial assessment
- [Careers](https://platform.drtutor.uk/careers): Join the Dr Tutor team as a tutor

## What Makes Dr Tutor Different

- Every tutor holds a PGCE qualification in science education
- Personalised lesson plans tailored to each student's learning style and curriculum goals
- "Lessons That Stick" methodology — structured teaching cycles designed for long-term retention
- Real-time progress tracking dashboards for parents
- Comprehensive online resource library included at no extra cost
- Flexible monthly packages with no lock-in contracts
- Diagnostic-led approach: every student starts with a free assessment to identify gaps

## Subjects & Key Stages Covered

- KS2 Science (ages 7–11): Building strong foundations in scientific enquiry
- KS3 Science (ages 11–14): Bridging primary to secondary curriculum
- GCSE Science (ages 14–16): Biology, Chemistry, Physics — all exam boards
- A-Level Science (ages 16–18): In-depth specialist tutoring for Biology, Chemistry, Physics
- 11+ Preparation: Science reasoning and exam technique
